title: Numbers: Rebellion at Kadesh
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of numbers: rebellion at kadesh and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study numbers: rebellion at kadesh and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: Why did God prevent the entire first generation of Israel from entering Canaan? Answer: because they believed the report of the ten spies and refused to enter the land, showing unbelief in God's promise.
segments.correct_answer: unbelief — they refused to enter Canaan after the ten spies' negative report
acceptable_variants: they believed the ten spies and rejected god's promise
because they failed to trust god after the spies' report

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The location where Israel rejected God's command to enter Canaan after the twelve spies' report; the site of judgment where the first generation was condemned to die in the wilderness
The two spies who gave a faithful report and urged Israel to enter Canaan; they alone of the first generation survived to enter the promised land
